penalties.description: The marked circle was not completely cut out. No cutting action was observed.

consensus_score.reasoning: Both models agreed that the marked circle was not completely cut out, but disagreed on whether any cutting action occurred. Claude reported no cutting action at all (0 seconds), while GPT reported 240 seconds of activity. Given the shared observation that only manipulation/positioning occurred without visible cutting instruments or cutting action, the task was never properly initiated beyond setup, resulting in zero completion time and zero score.

disagreements.rationale: Both models agreed no cutting action was performed and only manipulation occurred. If no task execution beyond setup was observed, completion time should be 0 rather than crediting time for non-task activity.
With 0 completion time, time_score = max_time - completion_time = 300 - 0 = 300, but since the task was never properly initiated, the effective score is 0.
When a task is never initiated and receives zero score due to non-completion, penalty deductions become moot as the total score is already zero.
Both models agreed the circle was not cut out and no cutting action occurred. This constitutes task non-completion, resulting in zero score regardless of time calculations.
